example_cases.description: readLayer returns parsed JSON for existing L0 file
example_cases.expected_output: { data: { updated_at: '...', languages: ['TypeScript'], ... }, valid: true, errors: [] }
example_cases.setup: Create .nopilot/profile/l0-infra.json with valid content matching schema

example_cases.description: readLayer returns null data for non-existent file (graceful degradation)
readLayer returns validation errors for schema-invalid JSON
example_cases.input: rootDir with l0-infra.json missing required 'updated_at' field
example_cases.expected_output: { data: {...}, valid: false, errors: ['missing required property: updated_at'] }
example_cases.setup: Create l0-infra.json with content missing updated_at

example_cases.description: writeLayer creates file with auto-injected updated_at
example_cases.expected_output: Written file contains updated_at as ISO 8601 timestamp, success: true

example_cases.description: writeLayer returns L2_DISABLED error when L2 is disabled in config
example_cases.input: { rootDir, layer: 'l2', data: {...} } with config.l2_enabled = false

example_cases.description: writeLayer validates data against schema before writing
writeLayer creates .nopilot/profile/ directory if not exists

example_cases.input: rootDir with l0-infra.json and l1-arch.json only
example_cases.expected_output: { exists: true, layers: { l0: true, l1: true, l2: false, l3: false } }
example_cases.setup: Create only l0 and l1 files

example_cases.description: profileExists returns false when no profile directory
example_cases.expected_output: { exists: false, layers: { l0: false, l1: false, l2: false, l3: false } }

example_cases.description: checkStaleness detects profile older than threshold
example_cases.input: rootDir with profile updated_at 48 hours ago, latest git commit 1 hour ago, threshold 24h
example_cases.setup: Create profile with old timestamp, git repo with recent commit

example_cases.description: checkStaleness returns not stale for fresh profile
example_cases.input: rootDir with profile updated_at 2 hours ago, latest git commit 1 hour ago, threshold 24h

example_cases.description: readConfig returns defaults when config file missing
readConfig merges partial config with defaults
example_cases.input: rootDir with config.json containing only { l2_enabled: false }

example_cases.description: ensureGitignore is idempotent when already present
readLayer completes within 2 seconds for typical profile
example_cases.setup: Create profile with realistic content size

example_cases.description: readLayer reads all four layer types correctly
example_cases.input: rootDir with all four layer files (l0, l1, l2, l3)
example_cases.expected_output: Each layer returns { data: non-null, valid: true } with correct content
example_cases.setup: Create all four layer files with valid schema-conforming content

property_cases.property: For any valid writeLayer call to layers l0/l1/l3, the call succeeds regardless of whether l2_enabled is true or false in config
For any writeLayer call, the specs/ directory contents remain identical before and after the call
